title: Laboratory technicians
description: Apply theory and principles of civil engineering in planning, designing, and overseeing construction and maintenance of structures and facilities under the direction of engineering staff or physical scientists. Apply theory and principles of environmental engineering to modify, test, and operate equipment and devices used in the prevention, control, and remediation of environmental problems, including waste treatment and site remediation, under the direction of engineering staff or scientists. May assist in the development of environmental remediation devices. Apply engineering theory and principles to problems of industrial layout or manufacturing production, usually under the direction of engineering staff. May perform time and motion studies on worker operations in a variety of industries for purposes such as establishing standard production rates or improving efficiency. (Based on 35 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Prepare samples or photomicrographs for testing and analysis.
Discuss test results and analyses with customers and communicate test results or technical information to patients or researchers.
Keep detailed logs of all work-related activities and input data into databases or logs.
Record test data and prepare reports, summaries, or charts that interpret test results and summarize test results for authorities.
Collect samples of gases, soils, water, industrial wastewater, or asbestos products for tests on pollution levels and analyze these samples.
Provide technical or program assistance on public health, environmental protection, or workplace safety issues.
Set up, maintain, calibrate, clean, and troubleshoot laboratory and field equipment, including sensors and controls.
Inspect workplaces for health and safety hazards and monitor emission control devices for compliance with regulations.
Analyze and interpret experimental or environmental data to produce reports and assist in environmental studies.
Develop or implement quality control and assurance programs to ensure accurate test performance and compliance with standards.
Examine specimens with microscopes to identify abnormalities, pathological conditions, or contaminants in biological samples.
Conduct research or assist in research, including the collection of samples and information for environmental or health studies.
Maintain files, including hazardous waste databases or chemical usage information, and ensure the proper documentation of specimens.
Train newly hired laboratory personnel and supervise laboratory staff to ensure performance of laboratory activities.
Perform regular maintenance and inspect laboratory equipment following safety regulations.
Identify tissue structures or cell components to be used in the diagnosis, prevention, or treatment of diseases.
Operate computerized laboratory equipment to dehydrate, decalcify, or microincinerate tissue samples and prepare substances such as reagents for histological specimens.
Perform tests by following physician instructions and analyze samples of biological material for chemical content or reaction.
Prepare or use prepared tissue specimens for teaching, research, or diagnostic purposes and prepare data summaries, reports, or analyses documenting research findings.
Perform procedures associated with histochemistry to prepare specimens for immunofluorescence or microscopy and stain tissue specimens with dyes or chemicals for visibility.
Resolve problems with laboratory equipment and instruments, such as microscopes and mass spectrometers, and maintain laboratory equipment to ensure operational readiness.
Collect blood or tissue samples from patients, observing principles of asepsis, and embed tissue specimens into paraffin wax blocks for further processing.
Analyze the results of tests or experiments to ensure conformity to specifications and conduct chemical analyses of body fluids, such as blood or urine.
Supervise, train, or direct other technicians or laboratory assistants engaged in laboratory testing and teach students or other staff.
Prepare standard volumetric solutions or reagents according to standardized formulas and compile and interpret results of tests and analyses.
Set up, clean, and maintain laboratory equipment and archive diagnostic material, such as histologic slides and blocks.
Monitor product quality to ensure compliance with standards and conduct chemical or physical laboratory tests to assist scientists in qualitative or quantitative analyses.
Develop, standardize, evaluate, or modify procedures, techniques, or tests used in the analysis of specimens or in medical laboratory experiments.
Provide technical information about test results to physicians, family members, or researchers and prepare chemical solutions following standardized formulas.
Collect or prepare solid or fluid samples for analysis and prepare and package environmental samples for shipping or testing.
Inspect or measure various materials, such as carbon nanotubes or polymers, using analytical techniques or tools.
Contribute written material or data for grant or patent applications.
Monitor and adjust production processes or equipment for quality and productivity, identifying opportunities for improvement.
Develop sustainable manufacturing technologies to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and minimize toxic material use or waste.
Develop production, inventory, or quality assurance programs to enhance manufacturing processes.
Follow policies and procedures for radiation workers to ensure personnel safety and monitor radiation levels.
Modify, devise, or maintain nuclear equipment and ensure its operational effectiveness.
Analyze environmental samples like air or water for contaminants and pollution levels, collecting necessary data.
Record laboratory or field data, compiling findings and preparing reports with observations and test results.
Conduct surveillance testing for safety of nuclear equipment and monitor equipment performance for maintenance needs.
Determine radioactive decontamination procedures based on contamination levels and media, decontaminating objects as required.
Prepare solutions, reagents, or sample formulations needed for laboratory work and ensure equipment is testing effectively.
Collect geospatial data and analyze remote sensing data to monitor environmental changes or compliance.
Integrate remotely sensed data with other geospatial data and prepare documentation for presentations.
Identify and troubleshoot equipment problems, ensuring proper operational protocols are followed during testing.
Participate in investigations of out-of-specification and recommend corrective actions according to guidelines.
Receive and inspect raw materials to ensure compliance with specifications.
Provide quality control data necessary for regulatory submissions and complete documentation to support testing procedures.
Write or revise standard quality control operating procedures while ensuring lab cleanliness and safety standards are maintained.
Coordinate testing with contract laboratories and vendors, and review data from contract laboratories to ensure accuracy and regulatory compliance.
Develop and qualify new testing methods and evaluate new technologies and methods to make recommendations regarding their use.
Participate in internal assessments and audits as required, and prepare reports documenting project activities and data.
Conduct routine and non-routine analyses of in-process materials, raw materials, or environmental samples, compiling laboratory test data.
Calibrate, validate, or maintain laboratory equipment and evaluate analytical methods to determine improvements.
Train other analysts to perform laboratory procedures and perform visual inspections of finished products to identify quality problems.
Inspect buildings for safety and quality, including verifying compliance with regulations and analysing construction methods.
Review and interpret plans, blueprints, or site layouts, ensuring compliance with legal requirements and safety regulations.
Identify corrective actions for compliance with applicable fire codes, laws, and regulations and conduct fire inspections or code compliance follow-ups.
Inspect and test fire protection systems, reviewing blueprints to ensure compliance with safety codes.
Identify energy consumption patterns and recommend energy-efficient technologies or alternate energy sources to improve efficiency.
Educate customers on energy efficiency and explain energy-saving measures, answering queries on costs and energy-efficient appliances.
Schedule work assignments, set priorities for staff, and recommend personnel actions related to performance and grievances.
Inspect properties and equipment for compliance with safety standards, investigating suspected hazards and fire code violations.
Provide emergency medical services, perform rescue functions, and assess fire conditions to determine crew requirements.
Supervise or instruct non-destructive testing trainees, document testing methods, and prepare reports on findings.
